results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE OBJECTIVE OF THE PROJECT IS TO CONDUCT RESEARCH TO INCREASE THE SENSITIVITY OF A PREVIOUSLY PROPOSED SENSOR THAT SHOWS PROMISE FOR PROVIDING CONTINUOUS MEASUREMENT OF LEAD LEVELS IN PREMISE PLUMBING. THE PROJECT WILL BE DEVELOPED WITH THE GOAL OF REPORTING SUMMARY DATA TO NEWARK SEWER AND WATER AND CDM SMITH TO INFORM THEIR DECISION-MAKING. ACTIVITIES:THE UNDERGRADUATE STUDENT TEAM WILL MENTOR HIGH SCHOOL STUDENTS TO DEVELOP THE SENSOR MODULE HOUSING. THE STEPS OF THE PROJECT INCLUDE (1) FABRICATE AN IMPROVED LEAD SENSOR BY VARYING THE GAP BETWEEN ELECTRODES AND APPLIED VOLTAGE, (2) INVESTIGATE VARIOUS SENSOR CONFIGURATIONS AND SIGNAL PROCESSING TECHNIQUES TO IMPROVE THE RELIABILITY OF READING, (3) DESIGN AN OPEN-SOURCE MODULE FOR THE SENSOR THAT CAN TRANSMIT DATA WIRELESSLY. (4) DEVELOP AN INTERFACE FOR COLLECTING AND REPORTING COLLECTED DATA.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:THE DELIVERABLE OF THE PROJECT WILL BE AN IMPROVED LEAD SENSOR AND OPEN-SOURCE WIRELESS MODULE FOR THE SENSOR. THE EXPECTED OUTCOMES OF THE PROJECT INCLUDE, WATER SAFETY FOR THE PEOPLE WITH THE GREATEST RISK OF HARMFUL LEAD EXPOSURE ; MORE CAREFULLY MANAGED CORROSION CONTROL DOSES, SUCH AS THE NUTRIENT ORTHOPHOSPHATE; AND ASSISTANCE WITH UTILITIES' PLANNING OF THEIR RESPONSES TO ELEVATED LEAD LEVELS STRATEGICALLY, SPENDING FEWER RESOURCES ON TESTING AND ALLOCATING MORE RESOURCES WHERE THEY ARE MOST NEEDED. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROJECT WILL BE, COMMUNITIES IN NEW JERSEY, VULNERABLE COMMUNITIES, WATER UTILITIES, AND PEOPLE IN AREAS AFFECTED BY LEAD CONTAMINATED DRINKING WATER.
